heading: Method of collecting tax
label: 5061 Method of collecting tax

L. 99–514Pub. L. 98–369, div. Asection 1881 of Pub. L. 99–514section 48 of this title, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–509, title VIII, § 8011(c)100 Stat. 1953Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Oct. 21, 1986Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 5054, 5703, and 5704 of this title] shall apply to removals during semimonthly periods ending on or after .December 31, 1986“(2).—Imported articles, etcSubparagraphs (B) and (C) of section 5703(b)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by this section), paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 5061(d) of such Code (as amended by this section), and the amendments made by subsections (a)(2) and (b)(2) [amending sections 5054 and 5704 of this title] shall apply to articles imported, entered for warehousing, or brought into the United States or a foreign trade zone after .December 15, 1986“(3).—Special rule for distilled spirits and tobacco for semimonthly period endingdecember 15, 1986With respect to remittances of—“(A)taxes imposed on distilled spirits by section 5001 or 7652 of such Code, and“(B)taxes imposed on tobacco products and cigarette papers and tubes by section 5701 or 7652 of such Code,for the semimonthly period ending , the last day for payment of such remittances shall be .December 15, 1986January 14, 1987“(4).—Treatment of smokeless tobacco in inventory onJune 30, 1986The tax imposed by section 5701(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall not apply to any smokeless tobacco which—“(A)on , was in the inventory of the manufacturer or importer, andJune 30, 1986“(B)on such date was in a form ready for sale.”

Transitional Rules Relating to Determination and Payment of Tax, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 96–39, title VIII, § 80893 Stat. 291Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 26, 1979Oct. 22, 1986“(a).—Liability for Payment of TaxExcept as otherwise provided in this section, the tax on all distilled spirits which have been withdrawn from bond on determination of tax and on which tax has not been paid by the close of , shall become due on , and shall be payable in accordance with section 5061 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954].December 31, 1979January 1, 1980“(b)Treatment of Controlled Stock and Bulk Wine.—“(1).—Election with respect to controlled stockThe proprietor of a distilled spirits plant may elect to convert any distilled spirits or wine which on , is controlled stock.January 1, 1980“(2).—Election with respect to wineThe proprietor of a distilled spirits plant may elect to convert any bulk wine which on , is on the premises of a distilled spirits plant.January 1, 1980“(3).—Effect of electionIf an election under paragraph (1) or (2) is in effect with respect to any controlled stock or wine—“(A)any distilled spirits, wine, or rectification tax previously paid or determined on such controlled stock or wine shall be abated or (without interest) credited or refunded under such regulations as the Secretary shall prescribe, and“(B)such controlled stock or wine shall be treated as distilled spirits or wine on which tax has not been paid or determined.“(4).—Making of electionsThe elections under this subsection shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe.“(c)Taxpaid Stock.—“(1).—Taxpaid stock may remain on bonded premises during 1980Section 5612(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (relating to forfeiture of taxpaid distilled spirits remaining on bonded premises) shall not apply during 1980.“(2).—Separation of taxpaid stockAll distilled spirits and wine on which tax has been paid and which are on the bonded premises of a distilled spirits plant shall be physically separated from other distilled spirits and wine. Such separation shall be by the use of separate tanks, rooms, or buildings, or by partitioning, or by such other methods as the Secretary finds will distinguish such distilled spirits and wine from other distilled spirits and wine on the bonded premises of the distilled spirits plant.“(d).—Return of Distilled Spirits Products Containing Taxpaid WineWith respect to distilled spirits returned to the bonded premises of distilled spirits plants during 1980, section 5008(c)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (relating to refunds for distilled spirits returned to bonded premises) shall be treated as including a reference to section 5041 of such Code.“(e).—Return of Distilled Spirits Products Containing Other Alcoholic IngredientsWith respect to distilled spirits to which alcoholic ingredients other than distilled spirits have been added and which have been withdrawn from a distilled spirits plant before , section 5215(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall apply only if such spirits are returned to the distilled spirits plant from which withdrawn.January 1, 1980“(f).—Secretary DefinedFor purposes of this section, the term ‘Secretary’ means the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate.”

Transitional Rules Relating to Determination and Payment of Tax, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 96–39, title VIII, § 80893 Stat. 291Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 26, 1979Oct. 22, 1986“(a).—Liability for Payment of TaxExcept as otherwise provided in this section, the tax on all distilled spirits which have been withdrawn from bond on determination of tax and on which tax has not been paid by the close of , shall become due on , and shall be payable in accordance with section 5061 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954].December 31, 1979January 1, 1980“(b)Treatment of Controlled Stock and Bulk Wine.—“(1).—Election with respect to controlled stockThe proprietor of a distilled spirits plant may elect to convert any distilled spirits or wine which on , is controlled stock.January 1, 1980“(2).—Election with respect to wineThe proprietor of a distilled spirits plant may elect to convert any bulk wine which on , is on the premises of a distilled spirits plant.January 1, 1980“(3).—Effect of electionIf an election under paragraph (1) or (2) is in effect with respect to any controlled stock or wine—“(A)any distilled spirits, wine, or rectification tax previously paid or determined on such controlled stock or wine shall be abated or (without interest) credited or refunded under such regulations as the Secretary shall prescribe, and“(B)such controlled stock or wine shall be treated as distilled spirits or wine on which tax has not been paid or determined.“(4).—Making of electionsThe elections under this subsection shall be made at such time and in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe.“(c)Taxpaid Stock.—“(1).—Taxpaid stock may remain on bonded premises during 1980Section 5612(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (relating to forfeiture of taxpaid distilled spirits remaining on bonded premises) shall not apply during 1980.“(2).—Separation of taxpaid stockAll distilled spirits and wine on which tax has been paid and which are on the bonded premises of a distilled spirits plant shall be physically separated from other distilled spirits and wine. Such separation shall be by the use of separate tanks, rooms, or buildings, or by partitioning, or by such other methods as the Secretary finds will distinguish such distilled spirits and wine from other distilled spirits and wine on the bonded premises of the distilled spirits plant.“(d).—Return of Distilled Spirits Products Containing Taxpaid WineWith respect to distilled spirits returned to the bonded premises of distilled spirits plants during 1980, section 5008(c)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (relating to refunds for distilled spirits returned to bonded premises) shall be treated as including a reference to section 5041 of such Code.“(e).—Return of Distilled Spirits Products Containing Other Alcoholic IngredientsWith respect to distilled spirits to which alcoholic ingredients other than distilled spirits have been added and which have been withdrawn from a distilled spirits plant before , section 5215(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall apply only if such spirits are returned to the distilled spirits plant from which withdrawn.January 1, 1980“(f).—Secretary DefinedFor purposes of this section, the term ‘Secretary’ means the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate.”

children.heading: Collection by return
children.label: a Collection by return
children.text: The taxes on distilled spirits, wines, and beer shall be collected on the basis of a return. The Secretary shall, by regulation, prescribe the period or event for which such return shall be filed, the time for filing such return, the information to be shown in such return, and the time for payment of such tax.
section 5006(c) or (d),
section 5041(f),
section 5043(a)(3),
section 5054(a)(3) or (4), or
section 5505(a),

children.heading: Import duties
children.label: c Import duties
children.text: The internal revenue taxes imposed by this part shall be in addition to any import duties unless such duties are specifically designated as being in lieu of internal revenue tax.

children.heading: Time for collecting tax on distilled spirits, wines, and beer
children.label: d Time for collecting tax on distilled spirits, wines, and beer

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, in the case of distilled spirits, wines, and beer to which this part applies (other than subsection (b) of this section) which are withdrawn under bond for deferred payment of tax, the last day for payment of such tax shall be the 14th day after the last day of the semimonthly period during which the withdrawal occurs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The last day for payment of tax shall be the 14th day after the last day of the semimonthly period during which the article is entered into the customs territory of the United States.

children.heading: Special rule for entry for warehousing
children.label: B Special rule for entry for warehousing
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (D), in the case of an entry for warehousing, the last day for payment of tax shall not be later than the 14th day after the last day of the semimonthly period during which the article is removed from the 1st such warehouse.

children.heading: Foreign trade zones
children.label: C Foreign trade zones
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (D) and in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, articles brought into a foreign trade zone shall, notwithstanding any other provision of law, be treated for purposes of this subsection as if such zone were a single customs warehouse.

children.heading: Exception for articles destined for export
children.label: D Exception for articles destined for export
children.text: Subparagraphs (B) and (C) shall not apply to any article which is shown to the satisfaction of the Secretary to be destined for export.

children.heading: Distilled spirits, wines, and beer brought into the United States from Puerto Rico
children.label: 3 Distilled spirits, wines, and beer brought into the United States from Puerto Rico
children.text: In the case of distilled spirits, wines, and beer which are brought into the United States (other than in bulk containers) from Puerto Rico, the last day for payment of tax shall be the 14th day after the last day of the semimonthly period during which the article is brought into the United States.

children.heading: Taxpayers liable for taxes of not more than $50,000
children.label: 4 Taxpayers liable for taxes of not more than $50,000

children.heading: More than $1,000 and not more than $50,000 in taxes
children.label: i More than $1,000 and not more than $50,000 in taxes
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), in the case of any taxpayer who reasonably expects to be liable for not more than $50,000 in taxes imposed with respect to distilled spirits, wines, and beer under subparts A, C, and D and section 7652 for the calendar year and who was liable for not more than $50,000 in such taxes in the preceding calendar year, the last day for the payment of tax on withdrawals, removals, and entries (and articles brought into the United States from Puerto Rico) shall be the 14th day after the last day of the calendar quarter during which the action giving rise to the imposition of such tax occurs.

children.heading: Not more than $1,000 in taxes
children.label: ii Not more than $1,000 in taxes
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer who reasonably expects to be liable for not more than $1,000 in taxes imposed with respect to distilled spirits, wines, and beer under subparts A, C, and D and section 7652 for the calendar year and who was liable for not more than $1,000 in such taxes in the preceding calendar year, the last day for the payment of tax on withdrawals, removals, and entries (and articles brought into the United States from Puerto Rico) shall be the 14th day after the last day of the calendar year.

children.heading: No application after limit exceeded
children.label: B No application after limit exceeded

children.heading: Exceeds $50,000 limit
children.label: i Exceeds $50,000 limit
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(i) shall not apply to any taxpayer for any portion of the calendar year following the first date on which the aggregate amount of tax due under subparts A, C, and D and section 7652 from such taxpayer during such calendar year exceeds $50,000, and any tax under such subparts which has not been paid on such date shall be due on the 14th day after the last day of the semimonthly period in which such date occurs.

children.heading: Exceeds $1,000 limit
children.label: ii Exceeds $1,000 limit
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(ii) shall not apply to any taxpayer for any portion of the calendar year following the first date on which the aggregate amount of tax due under subparts A, C, and D and section 7652 from such taxpayer during such calendar year exceeds $1,000, and any tax under such subparts which has not been paid on such date shall be due on the 14th day after the last day of the calendar quarter in which such date occurs.

children.heading: Calendar quarter
children.label: C Calendar quarter
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “calendar quarter” means the three-month period ending on March 31, June 30, September 30, or December 31.

children.heading: Special rule for tax due in September
children.label: 5 Special rule for tax due in September

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding the preceding provisions of this subsection, the taxes on distilled spirits, wines, and beer for the period beginning on September 16 and ending on September 26 shall be paid not later than September 29.

children.heading: Safe harbor
children.label: B Safe harbor
children.text: The requirement of subparagraph (A) shall be treated as met if the amount paid not later than September 29 is not less than ⁄ of the taxes on distilled spirits, wines, and beer for the period beginning on September 1 and ending on September 15.1115

children.heading: Taxpayers not required to use electronic funds transfer
children.label: C Taxpayers not required to use electronic funds transfer
children.text: In the case of payments not required to be made by electronic funds transfer, subparagraphs (A) and (B) shall be applied by substituting “September 25” for “September 26”, “September 28” for “September 29”, and “⅔” for “⁄”.1115

children.heading: Special rule where due date falls on Saturday, Sunday, or holiday
children.label: 6 Special rule where due date falls on Saturday, Sunday, or holiday
children.text: Notwithstanding section 7503, if, but for this paragraph, the due date under this subsection for payment of tax would fall on a Saturday, Sunday, or a legal holiday (within the meaning of section 7503), such due date shall be the immediately preceding day which is not a Saturday, Sunday, or such a holiday (or the immediately following day where the due date described in paragraph (5) falls on a Sunday).

children.heading: Payment by electronic fund transfer
children.label: e Payment by electronic fund transfer

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any person who in any 12-month period ending December 31, was liable for a gross amount equal to or exceeding $5,000,000 in taxes imposed on distilled spirits, wines, or beer by sections 5001, 5041, and 5051 (or 7652), respectively, shall pay such taxes during the succeeding calendar year by electronic fund transfer to a Federal Reserve Bank.

children.heading: Electronic fund transfer
children.label: 2 Electronic fund transfer
children.text: The term “electronic fund transfer” means any transfer of funds, other than a transaction originated by check, draft, or similar paper instrument, which is initiated through an electronic terminal, telephonic instrument, or computer or magnetic tape so as to order, instruct, or authorize a financial institution to debit or credit an account.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of a controlled group of corporations, all corporations which are component members of such group shall be treated as 1 taxpayer. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “controlled group of corporations” has the meaning given to such term by subsection (a) of section 1563, except that “more than 50 percent” shall be substituted for “at least 80 percent” each place it appears in such subsection.

children.heading: Controlled groups which include nonincorporated persons
children.label: B Controlled groups which include nonincorporated persons
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, principles similar to the principles of subparagraph (A) shall apply to a group of persons under common control where 1 or more of such persons is not a corporation.
